premise: In a future where criminal rehabilitation works by editing memories, a memory technician discovers that a death-row inmate's memories have already been edited — by someone using techniques that won't be invented for another decade. The edits are surgical, professional, and they're hiding something the inmate did that no one in the justice system wants found.

characters.name: Zara Okonkwo
characters.description: Memory technician, early 30s. Nigerian-British. Works in the rehabilitation wing, not the forensic side — she fixes people, doesn't interrogate them. Meticulous, empathetic, has a policy of not reading case files before memory work so she treats the person, not the crime.

characters.name: Inmate 4471 — Ellis
characters.description: Death-row inmate. Former corporate security consultant, mid-50s. Calm, cooperative, seems genuinely bewildered by his conviction. His unedited memories are spotless — too spotless. Doesn't know his memories have been altered, and learning the truth might destroy the constructed identity holding him together.

characters.name: Director Osei
characters.description: Head of the memory rehabilitation program. Built the system, wrote the protocols, trained Zara personally. Discovering that someone bypassed her system is both a professional crisis and a personal one — because the editing technique looks like her own early research, abandoned because it was too dangerous.
